violations.text: Chicken observed with dates exceeding the 7 day expiration date. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

The floor behind the cabinet located in the kitchen has an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

(Deli meats ) was observed thawing on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025
(Cold hold unit ) located in the (serving line ) area was not in good repair. Ambient temperature observed at 57DF. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

The (ceiling) in the (storage ) area is in poor repair. Observed holes The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Per PIC, work order has been submitted. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024
The (ceilings) in the (storage) area is in poor repair. Observed holes. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024
Dead mouse was observed on glue trap. PIC will inform custodian for removal. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024
Gasket on milk cooler was not in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/08/2023

The floors in the dish wash area are constructed of unsealed concrete, which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. In addition, some tiles in meal service area were observed cracked. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024

The ceiling in the storage area is in poor repair. Inspector observed a hole in the ceiling where wires are feed through. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 11/08/2023

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Staff will utilize 3-bay sink to wash and sanitize utensils until the dish machine has been serviced. A leak was also observed at the rear of the dishwasher at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 11/08/2023

Refrigerator door gaskets on milk cooler #1 were observed in poor repair. Hinge/latch on milk cooler #2 was observed loose. Ice cream cooler lid was observed cracked. New Violation. Correct By: 11/08/2023
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 11/08/2023
Mops must be allowed to air dry correctly after use. Mop observed leaning in mop sink at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 11/08/2023

The floors in the dish wash area are constructed of unsealed concrete, which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. In addition, some tiles in meal service area were observed cracked. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024
